title: Buchnera usafuensis
taxonomy: Buchnera usafuensis is a species of plant in the family Phyllobotryaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is a type of flowering plant. The classification of Buchnera usafuensis also includes its family rank, which is Phyllobotryaceae, and its order, which is Ericales. Further classification includes its family's subclass, which is Asteridae, and its class, which is Magnoliopsida. The plant's classification is further grouped into its kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species ranks.

intro: Buchnera usafuensis is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and West Africa, known for its medicinal properties and traditional use in folk medicine. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties, which contribute to its traditional use in treating fever, malaria, and other ailments. The bioactive constituents of Buchnera usafuensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In traditional medicine, Buchnera usafuensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be used in the form of a paste or ointment to treat various health conditions.
